agent_roles.planner: Owns subscriber_agent_spec.md and keeps the sprint aligned with subscriber personalization.
agent_roles.generator: Implements exactly one approved task per session.
agent_roles.evaluator: Reviews the sprint contract, runs QA, and blocks completion until the task passes.

evaluation_criteria: Subscriber personalization preserves current behavior when no overrides exist.
Per-subscriber persona and preferred-source overrides are applied deterministically.
Each task ends with an integration test and a clean commit.
session_contract: Run `pwd`, read `subscriber_agent_progress.md`, read `subscriber_agent_spec.md`, read `subscriber_agent_tasks.json`, and run `./subscriber_init.sh`.
Pick exactly one pending task whose dependencies are complete.
Draft or update the task contract, then get evaluator review before coding.
Implement only that task plus minimal prerequisite refactors.
Add or update the task's integration test before asking for evaluation.
Run the task's test command and any needed regression tests.
Get evaluator sign-off, then update task status, append progress, and commit.

tasks.title: Initialize the subscriber-personalization worktree harness
scope: Create worktree-scoped subscriber harness files instead of replacing inherited repo harness files
Create the first sprint contract under subscriber_agent_contracts/
evaluator_checks: Worktree-scoped harness artifacts match the subscriber-personalization workstream
The first implementation task is explicit and testable
done_when: The worktree has a durable subscriber-personalization harness
The next task can implement the runtime feature directly

tasks.title: Add subscriber-profile resolution and grouped personalized delivery
scope: Add config support for subscriber-level persona and preferred_sources overrides
Normalize resolved recipients into subscriber profile objects
Group recipients by effective profile during delivery
Apply per-subscriber preferred_sources filtering on top of globally enabled sources
Expose delivery_groups metadata so grouped generation is observable in tests
Keep cached newsletter reuse only for the non-personalized default path
evaluator_checks: Legacy recipient-only configs still deliver one digest as before
Different subscriber profiles can produce different delivered digests
Subscribers with the same effective profile share one generated digest group
preferred_sources narrowing works without bypassing the global source allowlist
done_when: Operators can configure persona and preferred_sources per subscriber in config
Delivery sends profile-specific digests end to end
A dedicated integration test proves the personalized behavior

tasks.title: Add audience-aware newsletter persistence for personalized delivery
scope: Persist multiple newsletters per date when profile inputs differ
Reuse cached newsletters for matching personalized groups
Extend telemetry/tracking persistence to audience-aware newsletters
evaluator_checks: Different profile groups do not reuse the same persisted newsletter
Matching profile groups do reuse persisted output
done_when: Personalized delivery can safely reuse persisted newsletters

tasks.title: Document subscriber personalization and operator caveats
scope: Call out the current preview and persistence caveats
done_when: Operators can configure the feature without reading code
